page: Amazon Web Services r7i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) for accelerated matrix and vector operations. Memory capacity is 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7i.16xlarge demonstrates strong performance across memory-intensive and computational workloads. Its DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-25% results in both cached and uncached memory bandwidth tests, with uncached read speeds reaching 216.54 GB/sec. The instance also excels in data manipulation tasks, scoring in the top 10% for Geekbench image processing and the top 25% for database operations, Redis SET operations (76.88 million operations per second), and static web serving throughput. Additionally, the hardware-assisted AMX instruction set supports efficient local LLM inference, processing prompts at 259.02 tokens per second on medium 7B models.

This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. However, it does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. Given its massive 512 GB memory footprint and strong database benchmark performance, the r7i.16xlarge is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) and a 105 MB L3 cache, it features 512.0 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 5 GHz. The instance lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage over a 25 Gbps network interface. Benchmarks indicate strong performance, particularly in memory bandwidth, Redis operations, and database tasks, supported by hardware-level accelerators like Intel AMX and AVX-512. This profile makes the r7i.16xlarge highly efficient for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets.
Strong memory bandwidth and database performance in benchmark testing.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics.
